sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 50-year-old male, presents to the clinic approximately 3-4 months after a heart attack, which resulted in the placement of a stent in his left anterior descending artery.
He reports that his medications are helping, but he is concerned about the number of medications he is taking.
He mentions that he feels dizzy when standing up quickly, which he attributes to either the heart attack or his medications.
He also reports that he has not been taking his cholesterol medication, Crestor, due to concerns about muscle aches.
He has a history of diabetes, which contributed to his heart attack, and is currently managing his blood sugar levels with diet and medication.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of coronary artery disease, with a heart attack 6 months ago, for which he underwent stent placement.
He also has stage 3 chronic kidney disease and diabetes.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms beyond his dizziness and concerns about his medications.
Current medications:
Aspirin 81mg daily
Brilinta 90mg twice daily
Crestor 20mg daily (refilled)
Metoprolol 50mg daily
Fish oil (dose and frequency not specified)
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 127/80, pulse rate not mentioned, oxygen saturation not mentioned, temperature not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's vital signs are within normal limits.
The doctor notes a slight 3/6 systolic ejection murmur at the left base, and some 1+ nonpitting edema in the lower extremities.
Test Results:
Kidney function (creatinine) is stable.
Assessment / Problem List:

Assessment: The patient is a 50-year-old male with coronary artery disease, stage 3 chronic kidney disease, and diabetes, who is doing well on his current medications but requires further management to optimize his condition.
Problem list: Coronary artery disease; stage 3 chronic kidney disease; diabetes; depression (suspected).
Plan:
I will refer the patient to cardiac rehab to increase his activity level and confidence in exercising.
I will refill his medications, including metoprolol 50mg daily, Crestor 20mg daily, and aspirin 81mg daily.
I will also refer him to a dietitian for education on managing his diabetes.
I will order another hemoglobin A1c to monitor his blood sugar levels.
I will contact Dr. Miller to schedule an appointment for the patient to follow up with his nephrologist.
I will also place a referral to psychiatry for the patient's difficulty adjusting to his heart attack and possible mild depression.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with his cardiologist in 3-6 months to monitor his condition and adjust his treatment plan as needed.
